channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Salasar Techno Hit by LPG Supply Disruptions Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Salasar Techno Engineering flagged operational challenges from LPG supply disruptions due to the Middle East conflict. The company cited force majeure conditions impacting production, material delivery, and its EPC business, as well as exports to the Gulf region. This comes despite strong Q4 FY25 financial results.

item.title: Geopolitical Jitters Widen Indian Rupee Exchange Rate Spreads
item.description: Indian Rupee (INR) exchange rates show wide differences for consumers based on transaction type, with banks adding markups. On March 12, 2026, inward remittances saw rates from ₹90.64-₹92.01 per USD, outward rates were higher at ₹92.67-₹93.99, and cash purchases could hit ₹95.05. These gaps stem from bank fees, geopolitical risks, high oil prices, and interest rate differences. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) intervenes to support the rupee.

item.title: Bank of Baroda Launches Primary Dealer Unit BOB Securities with ₹2,000 Cr Capital
item.description: Bank of Baroda is launching a wholly-owned subsidiary, BOB Securities & Giltedge Limited, to operate as a Standalone Primary Dealership (SPD). The subsidiary will have an authorized share capital of INR 2,000 Crores, with a planned infusion of the same amount in two tranches, following RBI approval. This strategic move signals BoB's expansion into the government securities market.

item.title: Encode Packaging: Auditor Resigns, Board Meets Amid Revenue Plunge
item.description: Encode Packaging India Limited's statutory auditors resigned effective March 13, 2026, citing 'preoccupation in other official work.' The company's board is meeting today to address the resignation. This marks another auditor change, raising governance concerns alongside a sharp FY25 revenue decline.

item.title: NIIT Learning Systems Sets March 18 Investor Meet on Growth Strategy
item.description: NIIT Learning Systems Limited is holding a virtual investor and analyst meeting on March 18, 2026, from 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM IST. Executive Director & CEO Sapnesh Lalla and other leaders will share updates following recent strategic acquisitions and corporate restructuring efforts. Investors are expected to focus on growth strategies and the market outlook.

item.title: India-US Trade Deal Advances Amid New US Tariff Probes
item.description: India's Commerce Ministry confirmed trade talks with the US are progressing toward a mutually beneficial agreement. A February 2026 framework outlined an interim deal to boost market access and lower tariffs on Indian goods. However, the US has also launched Section 301 investigations into alleged unfair trade practices and excess manufacturing capacity by 16 partners, including India. These probes could lead to new tariffs by Summer 2026, creating export uncertainty despite the interim pact's promise of increased competitiveness for Indian products against nations like China.

item.title: Axis Max Life Insurance Changes ID Number After Moving Office to Haryana
item.description: Max Financial Services' subsidiary, Axis Max Life Insurance, has updated its Corporate Identity Number (CIN) to U74899HR2000PLC143012. This administrative change follows the relocation of its registered office from Punjab to Haryana, as confirmed by the Registrar of Companies, Haryana. The update is a legal requirement and does not affect the company's operations or financial health.

item.title: Cella Space Board Approves Real Estate Expansion, Sets Shareholder Meet
item.description: Cella Space Limited's board has approved a new real estate acquisition and disposal strategy executed via its subsidiaries. The company announced it will hold an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) to gain shareholder approval for this move and to appoint Akhilesh Agarwal as a non-executive director. The resignation of the Company Secretary was also noted.

item.title: India Rations Commercial LPG to Ease Supply Fears as Oil Hits $100
item.description: India is temporarily rationing commercial LPG and approving alternative fuels like kerosene for eateries due to disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure. The government aims to curb panic buying and ease supply pressure, stating domestic supplies are stable. This action tests India's vulnerability to energy shocks and its diversification efforts as oil prices exceed $100 per barrel.

item.title: Rathi Steel: Delhi Court Frees ₹30.7 Lakh Property After Dismissing ED Case
item.description: Rathi Steel and Power Ltd. received major legal relief as a Delhi Special Judge dismissed an Enforcement Directorate money laundering complaint. The court found no 'proceeds of crime' were established, leading to the release of properties worth ₹30,72,380.

item.title: Prostarm Info Systems Secures ₹6.71 Cr SAIL Solar Order
item.description: Prostarm Info Systems Limited has secured a ₹6.71 crore Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) for the supply and installation of a 2 MW rooftop solar PV system. This significant order from a major Public Sector Undertaking (PSU) bolsters the company's project pipeline in the renewable energy sector and reinforces its capabilities in executing solar power projects.

item.title: HUL Shuts Trading Window March 16 for FY26 Earnings
item.description: Hindustan Unilever Limited (HUL) will close its trading window starting March 16, 2026. This is in preparation for the company's audited financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. The window will reopen 48 hours after the results are announced.

item.title: Ravindra Energy Guarantees ₹296 Cr for Associate EIM's YES Bank Loan
item.description: Ravindra Energy Limited has issued a corporate guarantee of ₹296 crore for its associate, Energy In Motion Limited (EIM), to secure a ₹328 crore credit facility from YES Bank. While this is a non-fund based contingent liability for REL with no immediate financial impact, it underscores support for EIM's operations in the EV sector. Investors will monitor EIM's performance.

item.title: Daya Hospital Secures ₹150 Cr on JCI Accreditation, Plans ₹500 Cr Expansion
item.description: Daya General Hospital has secured ₹150 crore in equity funding from Ascent Capital following its Joint Commission International (JCI) accreditation. This capital will fuel a ₹500 crore-plus, five-year expansion strategy to triple bed capacity to 1,500, establish a dedicated Oncology Super Specialty Centre, and develop new facilities. The accreditation signals strong quality standards, boosting investor confidence in Daya's growth within India's expanding healthcare market.

item.title: GMR Airports Wins Delhi Cargo Terminal 1 Deal Through 2036
item.description: GMR Airports Limited has received the Letter of Award (LOA) from Delhi International Airport Limited (DIAL) for the Delhi Cargo Terminal 1 Concession. The company will upgrade, modernize, and operate the terminal until 2036 under a revenue share model, projecting ₹340 crore in the first full year. This follows DIAL's termination of the previous concession with M/s Celebi Aviation India.

item.title: US Investigates India's Overproduction, Trade Talks Under Pressure
item.description: The U.S. Trade Representative has started investigations into 16 economies, including India, over concerns about overproduction and large trade surpluses. This probe targets key Indian export sectors like solar, petrochemicals, and steel, potentially increasing trade friction. India's commerce ministry states trade talks are ongoing, but the U.S. action could lead to new trade measures, affecting its relationship with the U.S.

item.title: NCL Industries: Promoter Buys Shares, Nudges Stake to 0.47%
item.description: NCL Industries disclosed that NCL Holdings (A&S) Ltd, part of the promoter group, acquired 19,829 shares on March 12, 2026. This slightly increased the promoter stake to 0.4708%. The modest acquisition signals continued promoter confidence in the building materials and cement firm, adhering to SEBI rules.

item.title: Dabur India gets NCLT nod to hold meetings for Sesa Care merger
item.description: Dabur India has received a significant procedural order from the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) dated March 12, 2026. The NCLT has directed the company to call meetings of its equity shareholders and unsecured creditors to approve the proposed Scheme of Amalgamation between Dabur India and Sesa Care Private Limited. This order is a crucial step in Dabur's strategy to bolster its hair oil segment by integrating Sesa Care's Ayurvedic brand portfolio.

item.title: R&B Denims Shareholders OK Share Split, Bonus Issue, Capital Boost
item.description: R&B Denims Limited convened its EOGM on March 13, 2026, where shareholders approved a 1:2 equity share sub-division, an increase in authorized capital, and a bonus share issuance. These moves aim to improve trading liquidity and enhance shareholder value, following a February board approval.

item.title: EKI Energy Names Yash Joshi Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
item.description: EKI Energy Services has appointed Yash Joshi as its new Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective March 13, 2026. He is designated as Key Managerial Personnel, strengthening the company's corporate governance and compliance.

item.title: Shanmuga Hospital Names Dr. Jamwal New Operations VP Reporting to CEO
item.description: Shanmuga Hospital Limited has appointed Dr. Mehak Jamwal as Assistant Vice President (AVP) of Operations, a newly created senior management position. Starting March 16, 2026, Dr. Jamwal will report directly to the CEO, signaling a strategic drive to enhance operational oversight and efficiency.

item.title: Deep Industries Partners Advait Greenergy on Green Hydrogen Projects
item.description: Deep Industries Limited (DEEP) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Advait Greenergy Private Limited (AGPL) to jointly bid for and execute Green Hydrogen projects. This move marks DEEP's diversification into the growing clean energy sector, aligning with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.

item.title: Brigade BuzzWorks Seals 550-Seat Hyderabad Office Deal with MyComplianceOffice
item.description: Brigade Enterprises' managed workspace arm, BuzzWorks, has inked a significant deal, leasing 550 seats in Hyderabad to MyComplianceOffice (MCO). This agreement boosts BuzzWorks' total leased office space in the city to 1.1 lakh sq ft, reinforcing its position in the competitive commercial real estate market. MCO will consolidate its product engineering and global operations within this new, largest India office.

item.title: Vishnu Prakash R Punglia Promoter Sells Shares in March 2026 for Company Funding
item.description: Promoter Pooja Punglia of Vishnu Prakash R Punglia Ltd has sold 10.95 lakh shares (0.88% stake) via market transactions on March 10-11, 2026. The sale, reducing her holding to 1.12%, aims to generate liquidity for infusion into the company, following recent promoter capital support and debt reduction initiatives.

item.title: Longspur International Plans ₹20.35 Cr Fundraise for Solar Push
item.description: Longspur International Ventures Limited will seek shareholder approval at its EGM on April 6, 2026, for a ₹20.35 crore preferential share issue and a significant diversification into the solar energy sector. The move aims to fund working capital and expand into renewable energy solutions, signaling a strategic shift for the company.

item.title: CIAN Agro: Shareholders Request Promoter Status Change
item.description: CIAN Agro Industries & Infrastructure Limited has received requests from ten shareholders, owning a combined 203,585 shares, to reclassify them from 'Promoter / Promoter Group' to 'Public'. The company's Board of Directors will review these requests at an upcoming meeting, following SEBI regulations. Further shareholder and BSE Limited approvals may be required.

item.title: India Proposes Higher Meal Voucher Tax Exemption, Pushing Digital Benefit Shift
item.description: Draft Indian tax rules propose raising the per-meal voucher exemption from Rs 50 to Rs 200, potentially boosting annual tax-free benefits to Rs 1,05,600. This aligns with a broader corporate trend of adopting structured digital benefits like fuel and corporate cards over cash, though adoption hinges on navigating the new tax regime and infrastructure availability.

item.title: Indo-City Infotech: Promoter Aneel Jain Boosts Stake to 19.20%
item.description: Promoter Aneel Jain of Indo-City Infotech bought 15,000 shares on March 12, 2026, boosting his stake to 19.20%. This purchase signals ongoing promoter confidence in the company.

item.title: Kilitch Drugs Shareholders OK 1:1 Bonus Shares, Doubling Holdings
item.description: Kilitch Drugs (India) Ltd. shareholders approved a 1:1 bonus share issue and an increase in authorized capital from ₹25 crore to ₹40 crore at an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) held on March 13, 2026. The move signals management's confidence and aims to reward existing shareholders by effectively doubling their holdings. The company is also advancing its Greenfield project, funded by a recent rights issue.

item.title: JSW Dulux GST Demand Slightly Lowered to ₹1.5 Crore After Appeal
item.description: JSW Dulux Limited, formerly Akzo Nobel India, has received an appeal order revising its Goods and Services Tax (GST) demand for fiscal years 2020-21 and 2021-22. The revised aggregate demand now stands at ₹1,50,31,077. While the demand is slightly lower than the initial assessment, the litigation is ongoing as the company prepares its response to the latest order.

item.title: NCL Industries Promoter Frees 9,500 Shares, Easing Pledge Worries
item.description: NCL Industries promoter G.T. Sandeep has released 9,500 equity shares from a pledge, a small step representing 0.02% of the company's total stock. While minor, this move may signal potential relief from financial encumbrances. It comes after a period of mixed promoter activity, including recent buying alongside past regulatory issues.

item.title: Karnataka Bank Halts Insider Trading April 1 for FY26 Results
item.description: Karnataka Bank is closing its trading window from April 1, 2026, until 48 hours after its FY26 audited financial results are announced. This aligns with SEBI insider trading rules, restricting designated employees, connected persons, and their families from trading bank shares to prevent misuse of sensitive information.

item.title: Midwest Gold Plans ₹200 Crore Share Sale for Rare Earth Magnet Facility
item.description: Midwest Gold Limited announced plans for a ₹200 crore preferential issue to fund its strategic diversification into Rare Earth Magnet manufacturing. An Extraordinary General Meeting (EOGM) is scheduled for March 14, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the issue, which is priced at Rs. 2,000 per share. The funds will also be used for debt repayment and augmenting working capital.

item.title: City Union Bank Expands Network with 5 New Branches, Total Reaches 925
item.description: City Union Bank has expanded its physical footprint by opening five new branches, operational from March 13, 2026. This strategic move increases the bank's total branch network to 925, aiming to enhance customer reach and accessibility across its operating regions. The expansion aligns with the bank's strategy to balance physical network growth with digital acceleration.

item.title: Golden Legand Leasing Launches Digital Payments Unit with ₹5 Crore Investment
item.description: Golden Legand Leasing and Finance Ltd. has launched a wholly-owned subsidiary, Gullakkart Private Limited, to expand into digital payments and Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPI). The company is investing an initial ₹5 crore into the new venture, marking its strategic entry into the fast-growing fintech market.

item.title: Muthoot Microfin Faces ₹177 Crore Short-Term Funding Mismatch
item.description: Muthoot Microfin has submitted its provisional Asset Liability Management (ALM) statement for February 2026. The filing highlights a ₹177.45 crore mismatch in the 0-1 year maturity bucket, indicating potential interest rate sensitivity and signaling the importance of liquidity management for the microfinance institution.

item.title: SJ Corporation Approves ₹42 Cr Preferential Allotment, Capital to Hit ₹4.34 Cr
item.description: SJ Corporation Ltd.'s Board of Directors has approved a preferential allotment of 3.5 crore equity shares at ₹12 per share, raising ₹42 crore. This will significantly boost the company's paid-up capital from ₹0.84 crore to ₹4.34 crore, strengthening its financial base. The allotment is to proposed promoters and non-promoters.

item.title: Jio Financial, Allianz JV Cleared to Launch Reinsurer in India
item.description: Allianz Jio Reinsurance Limited (AJRL), a 50:50 joint venture between Jio Financial Services (JFSL) and Allianz Europe B.V., has received its Certificate of Registration from the IRDAI, allowing it to commence operations as a reinsurer in India. This marks a significant step for JFSL into the high-growth Indian reinsurance sector, leveraging Allianz's global expertise and JFSL's local market reach.

item.title: Accedere Promoter Sells 5% Stake for SEBI Compliance
item.description: Accedere Limited's promoter, Mr. Ashwin Chaudhary, will divest up to 5% of his shareholding via open market sales between March 16-20, 2026. This move is crucial to comply with SEBI's Minimum Public Shareholding (MPS) regulations, aiming to increase the public float. An extension may be sought if the full divestment isn't completed within the initial period.

item.title: Arco Leasing: Kothari, Jaiswal Launch ₹10/Share Open Offer for 25.57% Stake
item.description: Acquirers Jitesh Kothari and Atul Jaiswal have launched a mandatory open offer for 25.57% of Arco Leasing Limited at ₹10 per share, totalling ₹2.77 crore. The deal, aimed at gaining control of the non-banking financial company, is contingent on Reserve Bank of India (RBI) approval. Current promoters will cease to be part of the promoter group.
